webhooks.name: clusterservingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure model format priorities are consistent (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format and protocol, and no conflicting priorities within a single runtime) and that multi-node WorkerSpec configurations are valid (e.g., no removal of existing WorkerSpec, single container limit, valid parallel size values, known GPU types, and disallowed environment variable overrides).

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that intercepts Pod creation for KServe InferenceService workloads, injecting storage initializer containers (for model downloading), credential/secret volume mounts, inference logger/batcher agent sidecars, metrics aggregator sidecars, GKE accelerator node selectors, and Istio CNI security contexts so that serving pods are fully configured at admission time.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService v1alpha1 resources on create and update, enforcing router/HTTPRoute cross-field constraints (refs vs inline spec, managed vs user-defined gateway compatibility), parallelism rules (pipeline and data parallelism mutual exclusivity, worker requires parallelism, positive values), scheduler config validity (replicas > 0, exactly one of inline or ref), workload scaling rules (delegated to v1alpha2), and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService v1alpha2 resources on create and update, enforcing cross-field constraints on router/gateway/HTTPRoute configuration, parallelism settings (pipeline vs data parallelism), immutability of parallelism size, scheduler config completeness, and autoscaling rules including WVA actuator consistency (HPA vs KEDA), replica bounds, variantCost format, and mutual exclusivity of scaling with static replicas or multi-node worker deployments.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, ensuring that the forbidden 'baseRefs' field is not set and that scheduler replicas (if specified) are greater than zero. It also emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by rejecting forbidden fields (baseRefs for recursive references), ensuring scheduler replicas are positive, running optional injected validation logic, and emitting warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure model format priorities are consistent (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format and protocol, and no conflicting priorities within a single runtime) and that multi-node WorkerSpec configurations are valid (e.g., no removal of existing WorkerSpec, single container limit, valid parallel size values, known GPU types, and disallowed environment variable overrides).

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates pods owned by KServe InferenceServices on creation by injecting storage initializer containers (for model downloading), credential volumes/env vars (for accessing model storage backends), inference logger/batcher agent sidecars, metrics aggregator sidecars, GKE accelerator node selectors, and Istio CNI security contexts. It reads configuration from the inferenceservice-config ConfigMap and the associated InferenceService to determine which sidecars and init containers to inject.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for the LLMInferenceService v1alpha1 custom resource on create and update. It enforces router/gateway cross-field constraints (e.g., HTTPRoute refs vs inline spec mutual exclusivity, managed vs user-defined gateway compatibility), parallelism configuration consistency (data/dataLocal pairing, pipeline vs data exclusivity, worker requiring parallelism), scheduler config validity (replicas > 0, inline xor ref), scaling rules (delegated to v1alpha2 hub validators including actuator backend consistency across decode and prefill workloads), and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService v1alpha2 resources on create and update, enforcing cross-field constraints on router/gateway/HTTPRoute configuration, parallelism settings (pipeline vs data), immutability of parallelism size, scheduler config completeness, and autoscaling rules including WVA actuator consistency between decode and prefill workloads, replica bounds, variantCost format, and KEDA field restrictions.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by rejecting specs that include the forbidden baseRefs field (recursive refs are disabled) and ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero; also emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting configs that use the forbidden baseRefs field (recursive refs are disabled) or specify invalid scheduler replicas (<= 0), and emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime create/update requests by ensuring no two enabled runtimes in the same namespace share the same model format priority, that model format priorities are consistent within a single runtime, and that multi-node WorkerSpec configurations (GPU types, parallel sizes, container counts, blocked env vars) are valid.

webhooks.name: notebooks-validation.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates updates to Notebook resources, specifically preventing removal of the MLflow instance annotation while the notebook is running. This guards against a broken state where MLflow environment variables persist in the pod but the associated RoleBinding has already been deleted, leaving the MLflow client without permissions.

webhooks.name: notebooks.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts Kubeflow Notebook create/update requests to inject operational infrastructure: a kube-rbac-proxy authentication sidecar, trusted CA certificate bundles, OpenShift cluster-wide proxy environment variables, pipeline runtime image mounts, Elyra/DSPA pipeline configuration secrets, Feast config volumes, MLflow tracking environment variables, and reconciliation-lock annotations. It also resolves container images from OpenShift ImageStreams and prevents unintended pod restarts by blocking webhook-only spec mutations on running notebooks until they are stopped or restarted.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific networking defaults. On OpenShift clusters (detected via Route API availability), it enables the secure trusted network annotation and strictly disables ingress (overriding user settings) to enforce Gateway API-only access with centralized authentication; on non-OpenShift clusters, it sets the secure trusted network annotation to false.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific networking defaults. On OpenShift clusters (detected via Route API availability), it enables the secure trusted network annotation and strictly disables ingress (overriding user settings) to enforce Gateway API-only access with centralized authentication; on non-OpenShift clusters, it sets the secure trusted network annotation to false.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates RayCluster resources on create and update, enforcing that the cluster name matches a DNS-compatible lowercase alphanumeric pattern and that all worker group names within the spec are unique.

webhooks.name: mmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Registers mutating and validating admission webhooks for ModelRegistry resources. The mutating webhook sets spec defaults (service route, database config cleanup, automatic OAuth proxy to kube-rbac-proxy migration, and removal of redundant default images), while the validating webhook enforces cluster-wide name uniqueness by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources, ensures the resource namespace matches the configured registries namespace, and requires at least one database (postgres or mysql) configuration.

webhooks.name: vmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Registers mutating and validating admission webhooks for ModelRegistry resources. The mutating webhook sets spec defaults (service route, database config cleanup, automatic OAuth proxy to kube-rbac-proxy migration, and removal of redundant default images), while the validating webhook enforces cluster-wide name uniqueness by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources, ensures the resource namespace matches the configured registries namespace, and requires at least one database (postgres or mysql) configuration.

webhooks.name: minferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating (defaulting) and validating webhooks for KServe InferenceGraph resources. The validator enforces that serviceURL references in graph node steps use cluster-internal hostnames only and target the same namespace as the InferenceGraph, preventing cross-namespace references and public hostname usage for security reasons (e.g., preventing Authorization header leakage).

webhooks.name: minferenceservice-v1beta1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates InferenceService creation by enforcing a name-length constraint and preventing deployment into the protected OpenDataHub applications namespace (determined by querying the DSCInitialization resource). The mutating defaulter is a no-op placeholder.

webhooks.name: mutating.pod.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates newly created Pods that have RAY_USE_TLS enabled on their kserve-container or worker-container by injecting TLS volumes, volume mounts, and an init container that copies Ray TLS certificates from a shared Secret into the pod, enabling encrypted communication within Ray clusters used for model serving.

webhooks.name: validating.isvc.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates InferenceService creation by enforcing a name-length constraint and preventing deployment into the protected OpenDataHub applications namespace (determined by querying the DSCInitialization resource). The mutating defaulter is a no-op placeholder.

webhooks.name: validating.nim.account.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates NIM Account creation by enforcing a singleton constraint: only one Account resource is allowed per namespace. On create, it lists existing Accounts in the target namespace and rejects the request if one already exists.

webhooks.name: vinferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating (defaulting) and validating webhooks for KServe InferenceGraph resources. The validator enforces that serviceURL references in graph node steps use cluster-internal hostnames only and target the same namespace as the InferenceGraph, preventing cross-namespace references and public hostname usage for security reasons (e.g., preventing Authorization header leakage).

webhooks.name: vogxserver.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for OGXServer custom resources on create and update. It enforces that the distribution name is known, provider IDs are unique, model resource provider references point to configured providers, and adoption annotations do not equal the CR name (which would cause Deployment name conflicts).

webhooks.name: mutate-pod.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that intercepts Pod create/update requests in Spark job namespaces, looks up the owning SparkApplication by label, and defaults the pod spec with settings from the SparkApplication CR—including volumes, environment variables, config maps, affinity, tolerations, node selectors, security contexts, GPU resources, sidecar/init containers, owner references, ports, memory limits, and scheduling configuration—differentiating between driver and executor pods.

webhooks.name: mutate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default values on SparkApplication resources during create and update operations. It only applies defaults (via the operator's webhook scheme) when the application is in a 'New' or 'Invalidating' state, ensuring that defaults are not re-applied to already-running or completed applications.

webhooks.name: validate-scheduledsparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates ScheduledSparkApplication resources on create and update, ensuring the metadata.name is a valid DNS-1035 label so that downstream Kubernetes Services and SparkApplication resources derived from the scheduled app can be created without naming failures.

webhooks.name: validate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for SparkApplication resources on create and update. It enforces that the application name is DNS-1035 compliant (to prevent downstream Service creation failures), prevents conflicting NodeSelector definitions at both the application and driver/executor levels, validates DriverIngressOptions for duplicate service ports or ingress URL formats, requires Spark >= 3.0.0 for pod template features, and optionally checks that requested resources do not exceed namespace ResourceQuotas.

webhooks.name: connection-notebook.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for Kubeflow Notebooks that validates and injects 'connection' secrets referenced in the opendatahub.io/connections annotation. It verifies each referenced secret exists in the notebook's namespace and that the requesting user has RBAC permission to get the secret, then patches the notebook's container envFrom to add or remove secretRef entries as connections are added or removed.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DSCInitialization v2 resources on create and delete. On create, it enforces a singleton constraint by denying creation if a DSCInitialization instance already exists. On delete, it prevents deletion of DSCInitialization while any DataScienceCluster instance still exists, ensuring the platform initialization config is not removed before dependent cluster resources.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default values on DataScienceCluster v2 resources during create and update. Specifically, when ModelRegistry is in Managed state and RegistriesNamespace is empty, it defaults RegistriesNamespace to the operator's predefined default namespace.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v2 resources on create and update. Enforces a singleton constraint by denying creation when another DataScienceCluster instance already exists, and rejects any DSC where the Kueue component has ManagementState set to Managed (which is no longer supported).

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DSCInitialization v1 resources to enforce singleton semantics (only one instance allowed) on creation, and prevents deletion while any DataScienceCluster instance still exists in the cluster.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that defaults fields on DataScienceCluster v1 resources during create and update. Specifically, when the ModelRegistry component is set to Managed and RegistriesNamespace is empty, it populates RegistriesNamespace with the default value to ensure model registries are deployed to a known namespace.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v1 admission requests: on create, enforces a singleton constraint (only one DSC instance allowed); on update, denies setting Kueue to Managed state and blocks v1 API updates when v2-only components are currently Managed (to prevent silent data loss during v1-to-v2 conversion where v2-only fields would be reset to Removed).

webhooks.name: hardwareprofile-notebook-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: Kserve component enabled OR Workbenches component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts create/update of Notebooks, InferenceServices, and LLMInferenceServices to inject hardware profile settings (resource requests/limits, nodeSelector, tolerations) from a referenced HardwareProfile CR specified via annotations, and cleans up those settings when the profile annotation is removed or changed.

webhooks.name: podmonitor-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates PodMonitor and ServiceMonitor resources on create/update by injecting the opendatahub.io/monitoring=true label when the resource's namespace is labeled for ODH monitoring and the cluster-scoped Monitoring CR exists (indicating monitoring is enabled). This ensures Prometheus discovers component monitors in ODH-managed namespaces without requiring each component to set the label explicitly.

webhooks.name: connection-llmisvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for LLMInferenceService (serving.kserve.io) resources that automatically injects data-connection details (model URI, image pull secrets, service account) into the spec based on an opendatahub.io/connections annotation referencing a Secret. On create it injects the connection; on update it detects changes and performs cleanup/replacement of the old connection before injecting the new one, supporting URI, S3, and OCI connection protocols.

webhooks.name: connection-isvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for KServe InferenceService resources that automatically injects data-connection configuration (S3 storage keys, OCI image pull secrets, or URI storage URIs) into the ISVC spec based on a platform-connection annotation, and cleans up or replaces injected fields when the annotation changes or is removed.

webhooks.name: vworkspace.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for Workspace resources on create and update. It fetches the referenced WorkspaceKind to verify that the Workspace's chosen imageConfig and podConfig IDs exist in that WorkspaceKind's allowed values, and validates pod metadata labels and annotations.

webhooks.name: vworkspacekind.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for WorkspaceKind resources that enforces structural correctness on create (imageConfig/podConfig values, ports, redirects, defaults, env vars, pod metadata), validates incremental changes on update (detecting changed/removed options, re-validating redirect cycles, and preventing removal of in-use imageConfig/podConfig options by lazily counting Workspace usage), and blocks deletion when the WorkspaceKind is still referenced by existing Workspaces or protected by a finalizer.

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it verifies that all referenced LocalModelNodeGroup resources exist. On delete, it prevents removal if any InferenceService in the namespace still references the cache via labels, ensuring cached local models are not deleted while in use.

webhooks.name: validator.pytorchjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PyTorchJob resources on create and update, enforcing DNS-1035 naming, run policy rules, elastic policy constraints (nProcPerNode deprecation, requiring Worker replicas), and replica spec requirements including valid replica types (Master/Worker), exactly 1 master replica, at least one container named 'pytorch' with a defined image.

webhooks.name: validator.jaxjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates JAXJob resources on create and update, ensuring the object name is a valid DNS-1035 label, that jaxReplicaSpecs are present and only use the 'Worker' replica type, that each replica spec has containers with images defined, and that at least one container is named 'jax'.

webhooks.name: validator.xgboostjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates XGBoostJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is DNS-1035 compliant, a Master replica type with exactly 1 replica exists, only Master and Worker replica types are used, every container has an image specified, and at least one container is named 'xgboost'.

webhooks.name: validator.paddlejob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PaddleJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, replica types are only Master or Worker, each replica spec has containers with images defined, and at least one container is named 'paddle'. On updates it also validates that the RunPolicy has not been changed in disallowed ways.

webhooks.name: validator.tfjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TFJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, each replica spec contains at least one container with an image and a default container named 'tensorflow', and that no more than one Chief or Master replica role is defined.

webhooks.name: validator.clustertrainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates ClusterTrainingRuntime resources on create and update by ensuring their replicated job specifications are well-formed, and emits a deprecation warning on create if the runtime is marked as deprecated via labels.

webhooks.name: validator.trainjob.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainJob resources on create and update by resolving the TrainJob's runtimeRef to a registered Runtime implementation and delegating validation to that runtime's ValidateObjects method, rejecting requests that reference unsupported runtimes.

webhooks.name: validator.trainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainingRuntime resources on create to ensure that replicated jobs with recognized ancestor labels (trainer, model-initializer, dataset-initializer) have exactly 1 replica and contain the required named container for that ancestor role. Update and delete operations are allowed without validation.

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist by fetching each LocalModelNodeGroup. On delete, it prevents removal if any InferenceService still references the cache by checking the status and fetching each associated InferenceService.
